sanskrit:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः।
प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ।।२७।।
transliteration:
sparśānkṛtvā bahir bāhyāṃś cakṣuś caivāntare bhruvoḥ |
prāṇāpānau samau kṛtvā nāsābhyantaracāriṇau ||27||
literal_meaning: Having shut out external contacts, keeping the eyes between the eyebrows, equalizing the inward- and outward-going breaths within the nostrils…
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a micro-protocol for instant self-regulation: withdraw attention from sensory pull, anchor gaze and breath, and the mind automatically settles.  It is the Vedic equivalent of a 30-second vagal-reset that down-regulates fight/flight and opens a non-reactive ‘observer’ mode.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative arousal seeking an evidence-based off-switch before deeper work can begin.
trigger_scenario: You’re about to walk into a high-stakes meeting; heart racing, mind jumping between slides and imagined criticisms.  You need a quick, invisible reset you can do in the elevator.
anchor_text: Close your eyes, center breath, watch thoughts pass like clouds.
